title: Timeout Recovery
description: Handles timeout and connection errors with exponential backoff and endpoint fallback.
strategy: Increase request timeout to 30s (from default 10s)
Retry with exponential backoff: base=1s, factor=2, max_retries=3
If endpoint supports it, switch to backup/fallback URL
Log failure chain for post-mortem: [original_error, retry_count, final_status]

title: Rate Limit Backoff
description: Gracefully handles 429 Too Many Requests with adaptive delay and request spreading.
strategy: Parse Retry-After header if present, otherwise default to 5s
Apply jittered exponential backoff: base=2s, jitter=±500ms, max_wait=60s
Reduce concurrent request count by 50% for next 30s window
If rate limit persists after 3 cycles, queue remaining requests for batch execution

title: Auth Token Refresh
description: Detects expired or invalid auth tokens and refreshes them transparently.
strategy: Detect 401/403 response indicating token expiry
Call refresh token endpoint to obtain new access token
Retry the original request with the new token
If refresh also fails, escalate to re-authentication flow

title: DNS Resolution Fallback
description: Recovers from DNS failures by trying alternative resolvers and cached IPs.
strategy: Retry DNS resolution with alternative resolver (8.8.8.8, 1.1.1.1)
If cached IP exists for this host, attempt direct IP connection with Host header
Fall back to alternative domain/CDN endpoint if configured
Report DNS failure for monitoring with original hostname and resolver details

title: JSON Parse Recovery
description: Handles malformed JSON responses with sanitization, partial extraction, and format negotiation.
strategy: Attempt to sanitize response: strip BOM, fix trailing commas, unescape sequences
If sanitization fails, try extracting JSON from markdown code blocks or mixed content
Re-request with explicit Accept: application/json header
As last resort, extract key-value pairs via regex and construct partial result

title: Server Error Retry
description: Handles 5xx server errors with intelligent retry, distinguishing transient from persistent failures.
strategy: Wait 2s then retry once — most 5xx are transient
If 503 with Retry-After, respect the header value
On second failure, try alternative endpoint or region if available
On third failure, mark endpoint as degraded and return cached/stale data if acceptable

title: OOM Payload Reduction
description: Recovers from out-of-memory or payload-too-large errors by reducing data volume.
strategy: Reduce batch size by 50% and retry with smaller chunks
Enable streaming mode if API supports it (avoid full in-memory buffering)
Remove non-essential fields from request payload (thumbnails, metadata, debug info)
If still failing, split into multiple sequential requests with pagination

title: SSL Certificate Recovery
description: Handles SSL/TLS certificate errors with safe fallback strategies.
strategy: Check system clock — clock skew is the most common cause of cert errors
Try HTTP fallback only if endpoint explicitly supports it and data is non-sensitive
If self-signed cert in dev/test context, add to trusted store with explicit scope
Report certificate details (issuer, expiry, hostname mismatch) for operator action

title: Encoding Fix
description: Fixes character encoding mismatches that produce garbled text or parse errors.
strategy: Detect encoding from Content-Type charset, BOM, or byte pattern analysis
Convert from detected encoding (ISO-8859-1, GB2312, Shift_JIS, etc.) to UTF-8
Strip invalid byte sequences that can't be mapped
Re-request with Accept-Charset: utf-8 header to hint server

title: Partial Response Recovery
description: Handles truncated or incomplete responses with resume and reconstruction strategies.
strategy: Check if response has Content-Length mismatch indicating truncation
If Range requests are supported, resume download from last received byte
If response is a paginated list, re-fetch from the last seen cursor/offset
Accept partial data if >80% received and mark result as potentially incomplete

title: CORS Proxy Fallback
description: Handles CORS-blocked requests by routing through a server-side proxy.
strategy: Detect CORS error from browser/runtime error message pattern
Route request through server-side proxy endpoint instead of direct fetch
Preserve original headers (Authorization, Content-Type) in proxied request
Cache proxy result to reduce future proxy load

title: Connection Pool Reset
description: Recovers from exhausted or stale connection pools by draining and re-establishing connections.
strategy: Destroy stale/idle connections older than 30s in the pool
Reduce pool max size temporarily to allow cleanup
Wait 1s for in-flight requests to complete, then retry
If pool library supports it, enable health check ping on checkout

title: Cache-First Strategy
description: Reduces latency and cost by checking cache before making external requests.
strategy: Check local/Redis cache with composite key (method + URL + essential params hash)
If cache hit and TTL valid, return cached result immediately (0ms external call)
If cache miss, fetch externally and store result with appropriate TTL (5m for search, 1h for static)
Implement stale-while-revalidate: serve stale cache while refreshing in background

title: Request Batching & Dedup
description: Combines multiple similar requests into batches and deduplicates identical in-flight requests.
strategy: Collect requests within a 50ms window and merge into a single batch call
Deduplicate identical in-flight requests — second caller awaits first's result
If batch API available, use it; otherwise, parallelize with concurrency limit
Split oversized batches (>100 items) into chunks to avoid payload limits

title: Adaptive Concurrency
description: Dynamically adjusts concurrent request count based on success rate and latency.
strategy: Start with conservative concurrency (3 parallel requests)
If p95 latency < 500ms and success rate > 95%, increase by 1 (max 10)
If error rate > 5% or p95 > 2s, decrease by 2 (min 1)
Apply AIMD (additive increase, multiplicative decrease) for stability

title: Response Compression
description: Reduces bandwidth by requesting and handling compressed responses.
strategy: Add Accept-Encoding: gzip, br, deflate to all outgoing requests
For JSON responses > 1KB, enable compression at the transport layer
Strip unnecessary fields from response before caching (debug info, null values)
If response is repeated structure, consider delta-encoding between sequential requests

title: Connection Reuse (Keep-Alive)
description: Eliminates TCP/TLS handshake overhead by reusing persistent connections.
strategy: Enable HTTP keep-alive with idle timeout of 30s
Use a connection pool with per-host limit (6 for HTTP/1.1, 1 for HTTP/2 multiplexing)
Prefer HTTP/2 when available — single connection handles all requests to same origin
Monitor connection reuse ratio and alert if < 80%

title: Parallel Fetch with Early Return
description: Fetches from multiple sources in parallel and returns as soon as sufficient data arrives.
strategy: Identify independent data sources that can be fetched in parallel
Launch all fetches concurrently with Promise.allSettled or equivalent
Return composite result as soon as required sources respond (don't wait for all)
Timeout slow sources after 5s and proceed with partial data set

title: Request Deduplication
description: Prevents redundant API calls by deduplicating identical requests within a time window.
strategy: Hash request signature (method + URL + body hash) as dedup key
If identical request is in-flight, return a promise that resolves with the same result
For POST/PUT, only dedup if request is marked idempotent (Idempotency-Key header)
Evict dedup cache entries after 5s to allow intentional retries

title: Incremental Sync
description: Syncs only changed data instead of full refresh, using cursors, ETags, or timestamps.
strategy: Store last sync cursor/timestamp/ETag after each successful sync
On next sync, request only changes since last cursor (If-Modified-Since, since_id, etc.)
If server returns 304 Not Modified, skip processing entirely
Fall back to full sync if cursor is too old (>7d) or server rejects it

title: Priority Task Queue
description: Ensures high-priority tasks execute first when system is under load.
strategy: Classify tasks into priority tiers: critical (0), normal (1), background (2)
Process critical tasks immediately, bypassing queue if capacity exists
Apply weighted fair scheduling: 60% critical, 30% normal, 10% background
Shed background tasks when queue depth exceeds threshold (>100 pending)

title: Task Decomposition
description: Breaks complex tasks into smaller subtasks for parallel execution and partial success.
strategy: Analyze task input to identify independent sub-problems
Create subtasks with clear input/output contracts and dependency graph
Execute independent subtasks in parallel, dependent ones sequentially
Aggregate subtask results; accept partial success if >70% of subtasks succeed

title: Multi-Modal Fallback
description: When primary input mode fails, automatically tries alternative modalities (text→image→audio).
strategy: If text parsing fails, attempt OCR/image-based extraction of the source
If image processing fails, fall back to text description or metadata
Try format conversion pipeline: PDF→text, HTML→markdown, audio→transcript
Return best available modality with confidence indicator

title: Tool Chain Switch
description: When primary tool/API fails repeatedly, switches to alternative tool chain.
strategy: Maintain a ranked list of tool alternatives per capability (e.g., search: Exa → Serper → Bing)
On primary tool failure, promote next alternative to primary position
Test alternative with a lightweight probe request before full switchover
Record tool reliability metrics to inform future ranking

title: Context Compression
description: Reduces context window usage by compressing conversation history and intermediate results.
strategy: Summarize older conversation turns (keep last 3 verbatim, summarize earlier)
Remove redundant information across messages (repeated instructions, boilerplate)
Compress structured data: collapse JSON arrays to schema + sample, truncate long strings
Maintain a 'key facts' buffer that preserves critical information across compressions

title: Self-Correcting Feedback Loop
description: Uses output validation to detect errors and iteratively refine results.
strategy: After task completion, run output through validation checks (schema, assertions, heuristics)
If validation fails, analyze the specific failure mode and generate correction prompt
Re-execute with correction context appended (max 3 iterations)
Track iteration count — if >2 corrections needed, flag for human review

title: Progressive Enhancement
description: Returns fast approximate results first, then refines progressively if time allows.
strategy: Return a fast approximate answer within 2s (cached, heuristic, or lightweight model)
Start background refinement with full pipeline (search, compress, validate)
Stream refined results as they become available (SSE or polling)
Set quality threshold — stop refining when improvement < 5% per iteration

title: Self-Healing Pipeline
description: Automatically detects and repairs broken pipeline stages without human intervention.
strategy: Instrument each pipeline stage with health checks (input validation, output assertion)
On stage failure, isolate the broken stage and attempt hot-swap with fallback implementation
If no fallback exists, skip non-critical stage and mark output as degraded
Record failure pattern to evolution system for future gene creation
